title: lanonia verrucosa
intro: Lanonia verrucosa, also known as the Rough Apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Australia, specifically in New South Wales and Queensland. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Herbal preparations of Lanonia verrucosa, such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, have been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support overall skin health.
